summary: Hotz critiques the Unabomber's anti-technology stance while agreeing that industrial-technological society is problematic. He proposes a new axis for evaluating technology: does it centralize or decentralize power? Using comma.ai vs Waymo as examples, he argues we should build self-contained, decentralized technology that doesn't require industrial systems or societal cooperation to function. He holds up biology as the ideal model—efficient, beautiful, non-toxic, and decentralized—while condemning companies like Twitter and Facebook as 'industry without technology.'
key_points: Technology should be evaluated on whether it centralizes or decentralizes power, not whether it's 'advanced'
Self-contained systems (comma.ai, Bitcoin) are superior to networked systems requiring societal cooperation (Waymo)
Biology represents ideal technology: 6GB of human DNA is efficient, beautiful, self-repairing, and decentralized
The smartphone is a marvel but the network and society needed to produce it are nightmares
If civilization collapsed, the most valuable relics would be self-contained devices that work without infrastructure
money_quotes: Genies do not go back in boxes.

A woman in a forest can create a supercomputer. This computer is off the grid, will last for 40+ years, has petaflops of compute power, and is self repairing. It exists in harmony with nature. It is non-toxic.
The smartphone is a marvel. The network, a nightmare. As well as the society needed to produce it.
What pathetic losers are building Twitter and Facebook. That's practically industry without technology. And they have the nerve to call themselves technology companies.
Ask yourself this. Am I building technology to further centralize or decentralize power?
Biology is non-toxic. Biology is efficient. Biology is beautiful. Biology is technology.
Build the wrong technology, and we will live in an eternal prison.
key_insight: The crucial question for any technology isn't how advanced it is, but whether it centralizes power into the hands of few or distributes it among many.
